sub_pixel_system.notes: Not documented; light gun shooter on rails with no player character movement

special_mechanics.name: Light Gun Aiming
special_mechanics.description: First-person light gun shooter. NES version uses light gun controller for aiming and shooting. Screen scrolls horizontally on rails; player character not directly controlled.

physics_values.notes: Rail-based shooter; no traditional player movement physics

special_mechanics.name: Automatic Fire
special_mechanics.description: Pulling trigger allows fully automatic fire. No documented projectile physics.

special_mechanics.name: Grenade Launch
special_mechanics.description: Button near muzzle launches grenade with wide blast radius against multiple targets. Limited grenades can be replenished.

tier_notes: Operation Wolf is a light gun rail shooter with no traditional platformer movement mechanics. The NES version uses light gun input (Zapper compatible) rather than character-based movement. No gravity, jump, or horizontal movement parameters are applicable to this game paradigm. All physics documentation is absent from available sources.
sources_used: tcrf - Operation Wolf (NES) from The Cutting Room Floor
